item.title: S&P 500 down 1.52%
item.description: The US stock market shows signs of weakness with S&P 500 down 1.52% to 6672.62.

item.title: Brent crude at $96.74
item.description: Brent crude oil price rises 5.18% to $96.74 due to the escalating Middle East conflict and concerns over oil production.

item.title: VIX at 27.29
item.description: The VIX index rises to 27.29, indicating increased risk aversion among investors due to uncertainty over the Middle East conflict.

item.title: TLT down 0.20%
item.description: Government bonds are in compression, with TLT down 0.20%.

item.title: DXY at 99.6300
item.description: The US dollar strengthens, with DXY up 0.40% to 99.6300.

item.title: Middle East conflict escalates
item.description: The joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran has led to Iranian retaliation, engulfing much of the Middle East in conflict.

item.title: Global inflation could rise
item.description: The rise in oil prices could lead to an increase in global inflation.

item.title: Oil prices rising
item.description: Oil prices are rising due to the escalating Middle East conflict and concerns over oil production.
